title: SBTA032 - Innate Immunity and Complement System Quiz

questions.question: Which characteristic is TRUE of innate immunity?
Innate immunity is primarily based on:
Which cells are specialized for phagocytosis?
PAMP stands for:
Pattern Recognition Receptors (PRRs) recognize:
Which of the following is NOT a mechanism of innate immunity?
The principal phagocytic cell of innate immunity is:
Which innate immune cell is the principal defender against parasites?
Mast cells release which inflammatory mediator during degranulation?
Which of the following is a chemical barrier of innate immunity?
The complement system consists of more than how many proteins?
Which organ produces most complement proteins?
Which complement pathway requires antibodies?
Which complement pathway is activated by Mannose-Binding Lectin (MBL)?
Which complement pathway does NOT require antibodies?
C3b primarily functions as:
Which complement component is the most powerful chemotactic factor?
The Membrane Attack Complex (MAC) is composed of:
options: C5b, C6, C7, C8 and C9
questions.answer: C5b, C6, C7, C8 and C9

questions.question: The final effect of the Membrane Attack Complex is:
Properdin (Factor P) stabilizes:
Define innate immunity.
questions.answer: Innate immunity is a non-specific, rapid immune response present from birth that does not develop immunological memory.

questions.question: What is phagocytosis?
questions.answer: The process by which phagocytic cells capture, engulf and destroy pathogens.

questions.question: Name the three complement activation pathways.
questions.answer: Classical pathway, Alternative pathway, Lectin pathway.

questions.question: What is the role of C3b?
questions.answer: C3b acts as an opsonin, coating pathogens and enhancing phagocytosis.

questions.question: What is the function of Natural Killer (NK) cells?
questions.answer: They kill virus-infected cells and abnormal (tumor) cells.

questions.question: Compare innate immunity and adaptive immunity, highlighting at least six differences.
Describe the sequence of events during phagocytosis and intracellular killing by neutrophils or macrophages.
Explain the classical complement pathway from activation to formation of the Membrane Attack Complex (MAC).
Describe the alternative complement pathway, including the roles of Factor B, Factor D and Properdin.
Discuss the biological functions of complement, including opsonization, inflammation, chemotaxis and cell lysis.
